reviewer: new-demo build wave (in-browser runnability proof + headless Chrome real download+inference on index + all 4 ladder pages, cached-reload auto-init probe, mobile+desktop overflow check, light/dark screenshots)

rubric.evidence: REAL in-browser inference verified before building: dynamic-import of @huggingface/transformers@3.7.5 in headless Chrome resolves AutoModelForXVector (bundle also exports WavLMForXVector + MODEL_FOR_AUDIO_XVECTOR_MAPPING_NAMES ['wavlm',['WavLMForXVector',...]]); loading Xenova/wavlm-base-plus-sv (q8) returned 512-D embeddings with cos(jfk,jfk)=1.0000 self-sanity. Headless build verify: fresh profile -> Download (~102 MB) -> ready -> Compare produced 0.915 'Likely the same speaker' (Voice1a vs Voice1b), embed A 1399 ms / B 1329 ms, WASM, 0 console errors, 0 net failures.
rubric.notes: There is no audio-xvector pipeline task in transformers.js, so the worker loads the model+processor directly (AutoModelForXVector + AutoProcessor) — no invented API. WebGPU is used when a real adapter exists; WASM otherwise.

rubric.evidence: Output shape + semantic sanity confirmed on a full sample matrix (embedded in-browser via the real processor): same-speaker pairs land high (Voice1a/Voice1b JFK 0.91, Voice2a/Voice2b 0.94), clearly-different pairs low (0.28-0.76), with a genuine confusable pair (Look-alike A/B, different speakers) at 0.88. Per page: basics 0.915 same; practical genuine probe ACCEPT at 0.915; wild ranked the line-up and flagged 1 FALSE MATCH (Look-alike B > threshold despite being a different speaker); multi-model raw 0.915 vs VAD-trimmed 0.892. The 512-D embeddings render as signed bars in 'See inside'; cosine + Euclidean distance shown numerically.
rubric.notes: SAME_THRESHOLD 0.86 (the WavLM-SV model-card cut) / MAYBE 0.80 separate the verified same/different bands; the confusable pair is presented honestly as a limitation in the Wild demo.

rubric.evidence: conformance declares-model-id=pass (Xenova/wavlm-base-plus-sv linked to its card), declares-task=pass (audio-classification / speaker-embedding), declares-backend=pass (WASM/WebGPU), declares-quantisation=pass (q8), inference-off-main-thread=pass (new Worker + worker.js). At-a-glance names the exact model, runtime (Transformers.js 3.7.5), 16 kHz mono input, [1,512] output, and honestly notes there is no audio-xvector pipeline — no invented API surface.
rubric.notes: q8 ONNX (~102 MB); a supplementary immutable io-shape assertion (speaker-embedding-semantic) was ADDED to record that the real output is a 512-D embedding, not class labels.

rubric.evidence: conformance shared-auto-init-loader=pass — every page loads through createModelLoader. Transformers.js caches the model under /Xenova/wavlm-base-plus-sv/ in transformers-cache, which lib/model-cache.js scans, so a returning visit auto-inits (checking -> initialising -> ready) with no Download button, and the per-model clear-cache control + storage readout work. First visit shows the honest Download (~102 MB) state — no silent large download.

conformance loads-clean=pass (0 console errors on load across index + 4 ladder pages), live-status-region=pass (role=status aria-live), errors-on-the-page=pass. Download progress streams (transformers.js progress_callback -> loader progress bar); failures surface in the .status.err line and the loader offers Retry. Mic capture is user-initiated and degrades honestly when unsupported.
rubric.notes: runs-without-webgpu-fallback: WASM path verified on the no-GPU headless runner; WebGPU is opportunistic with an automatic WASM fallback in the worker.

rubric.evidence: Every control exercised headless and is labelled + keyboard-operable: sample chips (role=button semantics via <button>, aria-pressed), dropzones + file inputs (Enter/Space), the user-initiated Record (mic) buttons, the Compare/Enrol/Check/Rank buttons, the practical + wild threshold sliders (re-decide from cached embeddings — no re-inference), and the wild reference <select>. buttons-have-names=pass.
rubric.notes: Threshold sliders recompute the accept/false-match decision from cached cosines — INP-safe.

rubric.evidence: All similarity numbers come from the on-device Transformers.js run — no canned values. The distinct, sensible cosines per page (0.915 same vs 0.28-0.76 different vs the 0.88 false match) prove the output tracks the real embeddings, not a placeholder.

conformance buttons-have-names=pass, images-have-alt=pass, live-status-region=pass. Waveform/gauge/embedding canvases carry role=img + aria-label; sample chips are real <button>s with aria-pressed; the loader status uses role=status aria-live=polite; mic buttons are labelled and disable honestly when MediaRecorder is unavailable. Landmarks + sequential headings per the accessibility guide.

conformance responsive-desktop=pass, responsive-mobile=pass. Measured mobile (360x740 DPR3) horizontal overflow = 0 px on index + all 4 ladder pages; desktop 1280x800 screenshots reviewed — the two voice columns use flex:1 1 260px + min-inline-size:0 and wrap cleanly, waveforms/gauge/embedding strips fit, chips wrap, no clipped controls. Dark + light both legible.

All feature extraction + inference run in a Web Worker (audio Float32Array transferred, zero-copy). The main thread only decodes audio (Web Audio OfflineAudioContext), draws waveforms, and computes a 512-element dot product for cosine — trivial. Threshold sliders and gallery ranking operate on cached embeddings with no re-inference, so no blocking loop. WASM embed latency ~1.3-2.7 s per clip on the headless runner.
rubric.notes: Guidance applied: break-up-long-tasks / identify-inp-causes (heavy work off the main thread).

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible labelled controls status live region keyboard operable chips mic recorder
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Prefer native elements (<button> for chips, not styled divs); give every control an accessible name and keyboard operation; announce async state in a role=status aria-live region; label non-text visualizations (canvas) with role=img + aria-label.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: Applied — sample chips are real <button> with aria-pressed; dropzones are role=button + Enter/Space; the loader/status use role=status aria-live=polite; waveform/gauge/embedding canvases carry role=img + aria-label; the mic button disables with a title when MediaRecorder is unsupported.

guidanceConsulted.query: responsive two-column voice panel no horizontal overflow on mobile flexbox
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use flexbox with flex:1 1 <basis> and set min-inline-size:0 on flex items with wide content so they shrink instead of overflowing; use gap + flex-wrap; prefer logical properties.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: Applied — the Voice A / Voice B columns use flex:1 1 260px with min-inline-size:0 and wrap on narrow viewports; waveform canvases are inline-size:100%. Verified 0 px horizontal overflow at 360x740 on all pages.

guidanceConsulted.query: keep INP low move heavy audio inference off main thread web worker
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Move long main-thread JavaScript (model inference) off the main thread into a worker so interactions stay responsive; don't run heavy loops during interactions.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: Applied — all WavLM feature extraction + inference run in a dedicated module worker; embeddings are transferred back and the main thread only does cheap cosine + waveform drawing. Threshold/ranking re-decide from cached embeddings (no re-inference).

openQuestions: WavLM-base-plus-sv WASM is ~1.3-2.7 s per clip; a WebGPU path could speed enrolment of many clips — worth a progressive-enhancement follow-up once verifiable on a GPU runner.

The bundled same-speaker pairs are two segments of one public-domain recording; sourcing two genuinely separate recordings per speaker (still rights-clean) would strengthen the 'robust to different sessions' story.

followUpGoals.goal: Add a live-diarisation ladder demo: segment a multi-speaker clip and cluster segments by speaker embedding, all on-device.
Add a WebGPU (ORT/transformers.js EP) progressive-enhancement path for faster batch enrolment once verifiable on a GPU runner.

summary: New demo: on-device speaker VERIFICATION / similarity via WavLM-SV (Xenova/wavlm-base-plus-sv, MIT) loaded through Transformers.js AutoModelForXVector in a worker. Index + 4 ladder pages (basics/practical/wild/multi-model), all verified with real in-browser inference (512-D embeddings, cosine), cached auto-init, clean console, and mobile+desktop parity. Framed honestly as voice similarity, not identity or surveillance; the Wild demo surfaces a real false-match. Sample voices are a public-domain JFK speech plus the showcase's existing ASR speech clips.
